name: Slurm GPU Binding Diagnostics
summary: Collect per-task GPU binding and visibility evidence from Slurm jobs.
description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose Slurm GPU allocation, per-task GPU binding, CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES remapping, and vendor telemetry before scaling multi-GPU training, simulation, or MPI workloads.

inputs.name: account
inputs.description: Site-specific Slurm account or allocation for the short GPU diagnostic job.

inputs.name: gpu_partition
inputs.description: Slurm GPU partition or queue for the diagnostic job.

inputs.name: gpu_request
inputs.description: Nodes, tasks per node, GPUs per node or per task, and optional GPU type constraints.

inputs.name: gpu_bind_policy
inputs.description: Slurm GPU binding policy such as verbose,closest or map_gpu:0,1.

inputs.name: application_command
inputs.description: Real multi-GPU command to substitute after the binding report is reviewed.

outputs.name: gpu_binding_plan
outputs.description: Dry-run srun command showing task count, GPU binding policy, and environment settings.

outputs.name: gpu_binding_report
outputs.description: Per-task report with Slurm GPU variables, CUDA/ROCm visibility, local task ids, and optional vendor telemetry.

outputs.name: gpu_topology_report
outputs.description: Optional nvidia-smi or rocm-smi topology and device inventory evidence captured from the allocation.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check shell syntax for the Slurm wrapper and per-task probe.
Submit the wrapper with RUN_GPU_BINDING_PROBE=0 to review the plan, then set RUN_GPU_BINDING_PROBE=1 on a short GPU partition and confirm gpu-binding.tsv has one row per Slurm task.
